column1: Review Text

Visited Sangrang on MI Road last week and picked up a few cotton kurtis. The fabric quality is genuinely impressive — soft, breathable, and perfect for Jaipur summers. Pricing felt fair for what you get. Will definitely come back for more traditional pieces.

Bought a 3 piece kurti pant dupatta set from here and honestly loved the whole experience. The staff was patient and helped me pick the right size. The block print design was beautiful. Great option if you're looking for ready-made ethnic wear in Jaipur.

Finally found a reliable place for daily wear ethnic kurtis that don't compromise on quality. The cotton is pure, the stitching is clean, and the designs are tasteful. Not overly flashy — just the kind of elegant everyday wear I prefer.

I was specifically looking for Jaipuri block print gowns and this store had an excellent variety. The prints were authentic and vibrant. Staff let me browse without hovering, which I appreciated. Bought two pieces and both fit perfectly right off the rack.

As someone who shops for women's ethnic wear regularly, I can say Sangrang's collection stands out. The anarkali gowns especially caught my eye — well-fitted, good fall, and the fabric drapes beautifully. Reasonable prices for such quality.

Picked up a few readymade ladies cotton kurtis for office. They're comfortable, easy to maintain, and look professional without being boring. The straight-cut styles worked well for me. Happy with the purchase overall.

My mother asked me to find a traditional Jaipur clothing brand and I'm glad I stopped at Sangrang. She loved the cotton suit sets — classic patterns, good weight fabric, and easy to pair with dupattas. Definitely coming back with her next time.

The premium rayon kurti sets here are worth every rupee. Smooth texture, rich colours, and the drape is flattering. I picked one for a family function and got so many compliments. The store itself is well-organised and easy to shop in.

Had a great shopping experience looking for festive wear ethnic gowns. The collection was diverse — from subtle prints to more elaborate designs. I appreciated that nothing felt overly gaudy. Found the perfect piece for Diwali at a very reasonable price.

Looking for casual cotton office wear kurtis and this place had exactly what I needed. The cuts are modern without losing the traditional feel. Fabric is comfortable even after long working hours. Good value for the quality provided.

I've been hunting for affordable designer kurtis in India that actually look good in person, not just online. Sangrang delivered. The in-store experience was smooth, the collection was updated, and I left with three pieces I genuinely love.

The ladies palazzo sets here are fantastic. Soft fabric, wide leg fit, and the prints are in line with current fashion trends. My daughter and I both found pieces we liked. The staff was friendly and genuinely helpful with suggestions.

Bought a pure cotton kurti set with dupatta for a wedding function. The quality was noticeably better than most readymade brands I've tried. The embroidery detailing was neat and the dupatta matched perfectly. Very satisfied with this purchase.

Shopping for comfortable summer cotton suit sets in Jaipur heat is serious business. This store gets it right — light fabric, breathable weaves, and styles that look put together without effort. Exactly what I needed for the season.

I was skeptical about buying from a new brand but Sangrang proved me wrong. The Jaipur cotton kurti with palazzo I picked up is superb — the print is clean, fabric holds up well after washing, and the fit is true to size.

Great stop for anyone wanting to buy Indian traditional dresses without the stress of tailoring or waiting. Everything is ready to wear, well-finished, and the range covers casual to semi-formal occasions. Happy customer here.

The designer cotton straight kurtis here are a good blend of traditional aesthetics and wearable style. Not overly ornate, just right for everyday wear or casual outings. The fabric felt premium and the pricing was fair.

Visited specifically for Jaipur ethnic wear and was impressed by the range. From simple printed cottons to heavily worked festive pieces, there's a lot to choose from. Staff helped narrow down options based on occasion. Good experience.

Stumbled upon this store while exploring MI Road and ended up buying three kurtis. All cotton, all comfortable, all beautifully printed. One of them had a lovely Jaipuri block print that looked very authentic. Definitely worth a visit.

Needed stylish ready-to-wear kurtis for a trip and had limited time to shop. The store made it easy — clear sections, helpful staff, and good stock. Got two pieces that fit well and packed easily. Ideal for travel-friendly ethnic wear.

The collection of ladies cotton tunic tops here is genuinely good. Light, versatile, and easy to pair. I wear mine with both trousers and jeans and they work for both. Good quality for the price and no fading after multiple washes.

Bought a premium quality traditional suit set as a gift for my aunt. She was very happy with the fabric and the overall finish. The packaging was neat too. Nice to find a brand that takes care of these little details.

If you're looking for the best kurti manufacturer in Jaipur, this place deserves serious consideration. The craftsmanship is consistent, the fabrics are genuine, and the designs reflect actual Rajasthani textile traditions. Very authentic experience.

The anarkali style gowns here are lovely. Long flowing silhouettes, vibrant prints, and comfortable fabric. I wore one to a relative's engagement and felt confident all evening. The price was much more reasonable than boutique alternatives.

Sangrang has become my go-to for ethnic wear shopping in Jaipur. Every visit I find something new. The cotton fabrics are genuinely pure and the range covers everything from casual dailywear to festive occasions. Very consistent quality.

Looking for a Jaipur wholesale kurtis manufacturer for my small boutique — a friend recommended this place and I'm glad she did. The bulk pricing is reasonable, quality is consistent, and the designs are updated with current trends.

Bought a 3 piece kurti set here and loved the coordinated look. The dupatta fabric matched the kurti beautifully and the pant was the right length. Well thought out design. Ready-made sets like this save so much time.

Great variety of trendy women's ethnic wear. I usually struggle to find kurtis that are both fashionable and comfortable, but this store manages both. The rayon fabric pieces especially look elegant and hold their shape well.

The fabrics here are noticeably higher quality than what you'd find in most mass market stores. Visited for everyday cotton kurtis and ended up buying a festive gown too. Both feel and look expensive despite being reasonably priced.

Asked the staff to help me pick between two designs and they gave genuinely useful feedback rather than just pushing a sale. That kind of honest service is rare. Bought both pieces anyway and am happy with both choices.

Wanted to find where to buy authentic Jaipur kurtis without getting overcharged as a tourist. This store had transparent pricing and the quality matched what was described. No pressure to buy. Refreshing retail experience.

The block print gown I bought here has become one of my favourite pieces. Hand block printing is a dying art and it's nice to see it used properly here. The colours are natural-looking and the overall garment quality is solid.

Picked up a few cotton kurtis for my mother and she's been wearing them regularly since. She says they're comfortable even in the afternoon heat and the colours haven't faded after several washes. That's the real test of quality.

Solid selection for anyone wanting ready made ethnic wear for women. No compromise on stitching quality — everything I bought was neatly finished with clean seams and no loose threads. Feels like well-made clothing, not fast fashion.

I travel to Jaipur a few times a year and always make a stop here for my ethnic wear needs. Consistent quality, updated collection, and the staff remembers regulars. That personal touch makes a real difference.

The rayon kurtis here have excellent drape. I bought a set with matching palazzo pants for a formal event and it worked beautifully. The colour didn't bleed in the first wash, which is always my test for fabric quality.

Needed elegant daily wear straight kurtis for my work wardrobe and found exactly what I was looking for. Clean lines, minimal embellishment, and comfortable fabric. They're professional enough for meetings and easy to move in all day.

Found a beautiful Jaipuri block print gown for my sister's birthday. She absolutely loved it. The print was detailed and genuine, not the sort of digital print that fades after two washes. Packaging was clean and the staff was helpful.

Sangrang on MI Road is honestly one of the better places I've shopped in Jaipur. The store is well-kept, the collection is fresh, and the staff doesn't follow you around. Just a pleasant, relaxed shopping experience.

Bought comfortable summer cotton suit sets here and they've been great through the hot months. Light fabric, easy to wash, and the colours are cheerful without being loud. Practical, stylish, and affordable — exactly what summer dressing should be.

The Sangrang clothing online presence is good but visiting in person is better. You get to feel the fabric and see the prints clearly. Both times I visited, the in-store experience was excellent and the staff was knowledgeable.

Bought a festive anarkali for my cousin's wedding and it drew a lot of appreciation. The embellishments were tasteful and the fabric had a good weight to it — not cheap or flimsy. Held up well through a full day of festivities.

I'm particular about cotton purity and the staff here actually knew about the fabric sourcing when I asked. That kind of product knowledge builds confidence. The pure cotton kurti I bought has been through many washes and still looks great.

MI Road has many clothing stores but this one consistently delivers. I've bought casual kurtis, festive gowns, and suit sets from here across multiple visits. Never been disappointed. Quality and value are both reliable.

The ladies palazzo sets I bought here are both comfortable and stylish. The fabric is lightweight and the fit is generous without looking shapeless. Paired with the matching kurta they look like a well-put-together outfit right out of the bag.

Picked up a few readymade ethnic kurtis as gifts for friends visiting India for the first time. Everyone loved theirs. The designs felt genuinely Indian without being costume-like. Good selection for gifting purposes too.

Was looking for affordable designer kurtis in India under a budget and managed to find beautiful pieces here. The pricing was honest and the staff helped me find options within my range without making me feel judged. Great experience.

The premium rayon kurti sets have a lovely sheen to them without looking overdressed. I wore one to a colleague's farewell dinner and received several compliments on the colour and cut. Well made and easy to dress up or down.

My go-to whenever I need ready-to-wear traditional outfits without the hassle of getting things stitched. The sizing is accurate, the finishing is neat, and the stock is updated regularly. Reliable brand for ethnic wear.

Sangrang Jaipur kurtis have a distinctly authentic feel compared to what you find in generic stores. The printing techniques, fabric choices, and embroidery styles all reflect the local craft tradition. Worth visiting for the real thing.

Found beautiful cotton straight kurtis here for office use. Simple embroidery, clean cuts, and easy-care fabric. They look polished without needing much styling. Exactly the kind of hassle-free wardrobe option I was looking for.

Bought a gown with block print detailing for a family wedding and it was a great decision. The print was rich and even across the fabric — a sign of quality work. Fabric fell beautifully and I was comfortable all evening.

The store's collection of Jaipur traditional clothing covers a good range of occasions and budgets. Whether you're shopping for casual daily wear or something more festive, there's something suitable here. Good diversity in their inventory.

I appreciate that the staff here gives honest sizing advice rather than just saying everything fits. Got steered toward the right size and the kurti fit perfectly. That kind of genuine help makes shopping actually enjoyable.

Tried the 3 piece kurti pant dupatta set and found it to be a complete, well-coordinated outfit. The proportions were good and the fabric across all three pieces felt consistent. No compromises on the dupatta fabric like some brands do.

Great place to shop for daily wear ethnic kurtis that are easy on the wallet without feeling cheap. The cotton quality is good, the prints are crisp, and the range is large enough that you don't see the same design on everyone.

The store layout makes browsing easy. Kurtis are organized by fabric and style, which saves a lot of time. Picked up two cotton pieces and a rayon set in under thirty minutes. Efficient and enjoyable shopping experience.

Visited for the first time on a recommendation and was genuinely impressed. The Jaipur ethnic wear online shopping option is convenient but being here in person let me appreciate the fabric textures much better. Definitely worth visiting.

Bought a festive wear ethnic gown as a Diwali outfit and couldn't have been happier. The embroidery was delicate and the fabric had a premium feel. Comfortable enough to wear for hours without feeling restricted. Excellent choice for festive dressing.

The designers here clearly understand both traditional aesthetics and modern preferences. The kurtis have the right balance — heritage craft with contemporary silhouettes. That's a balance not every brand manages to strike.

Pure cotton in Jaipur summer is non-negotiable. The kurtis here are genuinely breathable and don't hold heat. I bought a few for my mother and she's been in them all summer. Practical, affordable, and good looking.

Casual cotton office wear kurtis from here have held up beautifully over several months of regular use. The colours are still vibrant, the fabric isn't pilling, and the cuts still look sharp. Worth every rupee spent.

I specifically look for premium quality traditional suit sets when gifting relatives, and this is now my default choice. The presentation is good, the quality is consistent, and the range ensures I can always find something appropriate.

Asked about the fabric sourcing and the staff gave a proper answer — they know their materials. That reassured me before buying. The cotton I purchased turned out to be exactly as described: soft, durable, and true in colour.

The best kurti manufacturer in Jaipur tag is well-earned based on my experience. Consistent stitching, quality fabric, and designs that actually reflect Rajasthani craft rather than generic prints. Happy with multiple purchases from here.

The trendy women's ethnic wear collection here isn't just trendy in a fleeting way — the pieces are rooted in proper design sensibility. I find myself reaching for my kurtis from here more than anything else in my wardrobe.

Bought two readymade ladies cotton kurtis for summer and both have been great. Light enough for afternoon heat but substantial enough to look well-dressed. Easy to wash and maintain. Perfect summer staple.

The palazzo sets here are well-cut and fall nicely. I'm tall and often struggle with length in readymade clothes, but the ones here were just right. It's nice to find ethnic wear that works for different body proportions.

Visited as part of a day of shopping on MI Road and ended up spending the most time here. The range of Jaipur kurtis was impressive and the prices were the most competitive I found anywhere that day. Came home with five pieces.

The rayon kurtis drape so well compared to what I usually find in readymade shops. The fabric feels substantial and the colour range is sophisticated — not just the usual brights. Found a lovely deep teal that looked stunning.

Looked up best brand for ladies cotton suits before my trip and this name came up multiple times. Lived up to the reputation. The cotton suits here are properly done — good fabric, neat tailoring, and classic Rajasthani patterns.

The block print gowns here have that genuine handcrafted quality that machine-printed alternatives lack. You can see the slight variation that comes with hand printing, which makes each piece feel unique. Bought two and love both.

Wanted to find stylish ready-to-wear kurtis that didn't look like they came off a production line. Sangrang's designs have personality and individuality. The collection felt curated rather than mass-produced.

Good experience overall — the store was clean and organised, the staff was polite, and the collection was wide. Found several options that I liked and the checkout was quick. No complaints whatsoever.

My daughter convinced me to try Jaipur ethnic wear online shopping but I preferred to visit in person. Glad I did. The fabric quality is something you need to feel. The cotton suit sets were lovely and I bought three.

The festive gowns here are genuinely suitable for celebratory occasions — rich fabrics, detailed work, and good silhouettes. Not the sort of thing you'd wear every day but absolutely worth the price for special occasions.

I buy comfortable summer cotton suit sets every year before the hot season and this is now where I come for them. The fabric stays cool, washes well, and the designs are cheerful without being childish. Reliable annual purchase.

Tried on a few anarkali style gowns before settling on one with a subtle floral print. The fit was good, the fabric moved beautifully when I walked, and the colours were earthy and flattering. Very happy with the choice.

The Sangrang clothing collection feels genuinely thought out — not just generic ethnic wear thrown together. Each piece has its own character and the overall range shows a clear design direction. Appreciated the cohesion.

Bought a cotton kurti set with dupatta as an anniversary gift and my wife loved it. The packaging was clean and the quality was evident. She's worn it several times already which says everything about how much she likes it.

Elegant daily wear straight kurtis are surprisingly hard to find — most are either too ornate or too plain. The ones here hit the right note: a little embroidery, clean lines, and comfortable fabric. Exactly what I needed.

The store stocks a solid range across price points. I found pieces around 500 rupees and others around 2000, and the quality difference was proportional — nothing felt like a rip-off at any level. Good honest pricing.

Visited specifically to find designer cotton straight kurtis and found a great selection. The cotton was soft and the designs were refined — subtle prints, clean embroidery, nothing overdone. Bought two for office and one for casual wear.

The Jaipur cotton kurti with palazzo I purchased has become a regular in my wardrobe. Comfortable to wear all day, easy to wash at home, and the printed fabric still looks fresh. Good quality that lasts.

Ladies cotton tunic tops here are perfect for casual outings. Long enough to wear with leggings, structured enough to look put together. The fabric is soft and the colours are washfast. Practical and stylish.

Wanted to buy Indian traditional dresses online but decided to visit first. The store experience converted me into a regular buyer. The sheer range and genuine quality make it the kind of place you want to return to.

I've gifted kurtis from here twice now and both times the recipients have been genuinely happy. The quality is evident and the designs feel special. Makes a thoughtful, useful gift that doesn't feel generic.
The staff here went out of their way to help me find a kurta that matched a dupatta I already owned. That kind of extra effort is what makes a store memorable. Appreciated the genuine helpfulness.

Bought several casual kurtis for travel and they've held up brilliantly. Easy to pack, quick to wash, and they look good after multiple wears. The cotton quality keeps them fresh-looking even after long days of sightseeing.

The three-piece sets here are worth it for the coordination alone. Everything matches — print, colour tone, and fabric — which takes the guesswork out of dressing for occasions. Practical and polished at the same time.

First visit to Sangrang on MI Road and I was pleasantly surprised. Didn't expect such a well-organized store with so many distinct styles. Ended up buying more than I planned. A genuinely enjoyable shopping experience.

Looking for Jaipur traditional clothing for my sister's haldi ceremony and found a gorgeous yellow block print set here. The fabric was light and the print was authentic. Exactly the aesthetic we wanted. Very happy.

Readymade ladies cotton kurtis here are just right for regular office wear. Easy to iron, comfortable throughout the day, and they look professional. Good investment for working women who want ethnic wear without the fuss.

The variety of Jaipuri block print gowns here is impressive. Several different block motifs, multiple colour schemes, and varied silhouettes. It took me a while to decide because there were so many good options. Finally settled on two.
Came looking for one outfit and left with four. That's what happens when the quality is good and the prices are fair. Every piece I bought has been worn multiple times already. No regrets.

The premium rayon kurti I bought has a lovely fluid drape. Doesn't crease easily, looks polished, and the colour is rich. Wore it to a formal lunch and felt appropriately dressed without being overdressed. Good versatile piece.

Asked the staff about the difference between a few fabric options and got a genuinely informative answer. That kind of product knowledge is rare in retail and it helped me make a better purchase decision. Good team.

The pure cotton here is noticeably different from synthetic blends often sold as cotton. Breathable, absorbent, and soft against the skin. If you're buying summer clothing in Jaipur, this is the fabric to look for.

Bought a festive anarkali for my mother and she looked wonderful in it. The embellishments were tasteful and the fabric had an appropriate festive feel without being garish. Good choice for traditional celebrations.

Nice to find a brand where the everyday kurtis get as much design attention as the festive wear. Too often casual pieces are boring filler. Here even the simple cotton kurtis have interesting details and colour combinations.

The ready-to-wear ethnic gowns here are well-finished and the lengths are right for different heights. I'm petite and the sizing still worked well. Not always the case with readymade ethnic wear, so this was a pleasant surprise.

Came for comfortable summer cotton suits and found the perfect set — a light printed cotton kurta with matching palazzo and a sheer dupatta. Everything together looked coordinated and the fabric kept me cool. Perfect summer purchase.

The store on MI Road is well-stocked and well-kept. The clothes are displayed clearly and there's enough space to browse without feeling crowded. The changing room was clean and the staff gave privacy. Good environment overall.

The affordable designer kurtis here look like they cost much more than they do. Good quality materials, thoughtful designs, and neat finishing. Value for money in the truest sense — you get more than what you pay for.

My cousin introduced me to this place and I've now become a regular. Mostly come for the cotton kurtis but have also picked up rayon sets and palazzo sets over time. Never had a bad purchase from here.

The Jaipur ethnic wear collection is genuinely representative of local textile traditions. Block prints, Bagru dyes, and traditional silhouettes — not just generic Indian clothing with a Jaipur label. The authenticity is evident.

Staff helped me pick between two similar pieces with genuinely useful feedback on what would work better for the occasion I mentioned. That kind of attentive service goes a long way. Left with the right choice.

The cotton fabric in the kurtis I bought has been through many washes and still looks and feels good. No pilling, no significant shrinkage, and the colours are still vibrant. That's the mark of quality cotton sourcing.

Bought a Jaipuri block print gown for a mehendi ceremony and loved how it looked on the day. The print complemented the festive setting perfectly and the fabric moved beautifully. Got so many questions about where I bought it.

The 3 piece kurti pant dupatta sets here are priced well for what you get. The three pieces are clearly designed to go together — same design language, same fabric type. It's a complete outfit without any effort.

Elegant and comfortable — that's how I'd describe the daily wear kurtis from here. They're not boring but they're not trying too hard either. Just well-designed, well-made clothing for real everyday use.

Found the best selection of traditional suit sets I've seen in one store in Jaipur. Multiple fabric options, many print styles, and a range that covers casual through formal. Spent a good hour just browsing. Came away very happy.

The ladies palazzo sets here pair perfectly with the kurtis. Matching waistband elastics, consistent fabric quality across the set, and good proportions. I appreciate when brands put as much thought into the bottoms as the tops.

Sangrang Jaipur kurtis have become my default gifting choice for relatives who appreciate traditional clothing. The quality is consistent, the range is wide, and the packaging is presentable. Reliable across multiple purchases.

Visited to browse and ended up buying a beautiful rayon anarkali gown. The colour was a deep coral that looked lovely. Well-fitted off the rack and the fabric had a good weight to it. Delighted with the purchase.

The block prints here look genuinely handcrafted, not digital reproductions. The slight imperfections in the print are exactly what give them character. Bought two gowns with different motifs and both are wonderful pieces.

Looking for ready made ethnic wear for women that doesn't need alteration is always a challenge. The sizing here is accurate enough that I could buy straight off the rack. Saved a lot of time and money on tailoring.

The designer cotton straight kurtis are simple enough to wear regularly but elevated enough to wear to functions. That kind of versatility is exactly what I need in my wardrobe. Very practical purchase.

Great experience from start to finish. The store was well-lit and easy to navigate, the staff was responsive without being pushy, and the quality of what I bought exceeded my expectations. Will definitely recommend.

Specifically came looking for Jaipur wholesale kurtis for my boutique. The minimum order quantities were reasonable and the quality was what I'd expect based on the retail pieces. Good option for small business buyers.

The summer cotton kurtis here are genuinely breathable. Not just labelled cotton — they actually feel cool and comfortable in warm weather. Bought several for a summer trip and wore them almost every day.

The finishing on the kurtis here is genuinely impressive for readymade clothing. Clean inner seams, properly secured embellishments, and consistent stitching throughout. This is what separates quality brands from the rest.

Bought a festive wear gown here for my daughter's school function and it was perfect — festive enough to stand out, comfortable enough for her to run around in, and easy to wash. Exactly what a parent needs.

Came across Sangrang on MI Road while visiting Jaipur for the first time. The authentic Rajasthani textile aesthetic was exactly what I was hoping to find. Left with several pieces as souvenirs and personal keepsakes.

The premium quality traditional suit sets here are worthy of festive occasions. Rich fabric, neat embroidery, and the kind of finish that photographs well. Bought one for a family celebration and was very pleased.

The trendy women's ethnic wear collection updates regularly. Each time I visit I find new styles that reflect current fashion while keeping the traditional Jaipur aesthetic intact. That balance is hard to achieve.

I prefer pure cotton for daily wear and the selection here is genuinely wide. Different weights, different weaves, different print styles — all genuine cotton. It's rare to find this range in one store.

The anarkali gowns here have a regal quality without being impractical. Comfortable fabric, good length, and the embellishments are placed so they don't dig in or snag. Thoughtful design for wearability.

The Sangrang clothing range online looks impressive but shopping in store is even better. The photos don't fully capture the fabric quality or the depth of the print colours. Worth visiting if you're in Jaipur.

Bought casual cotton office wear kurtis here and they've been my wardrobe staple since. Easy to style, comfortable through long days, and professional enough for client meetings. High utility, good value.

Found the most beautiful pure cotton kurti set with dupatta here. The dupatta fabric matched perfectly and the print was consistent across all three pieces. Love when sets are genuinely coordinated rather than just colour-matched.

The affordable pricing doesn't mean compromised quality here. I've been consistently impressed by how well the cotton pieces hold up over time. They wash well, keep their shape, and the prints stay vivid. Good investment.

The staff had patience with me while I took my time deciding. Tried on multiple styles and wasn't rushed. Eventually picked two pieces I was genuinely sure about. That kind of unhurried service is what I prefer.

This store has become my preferred destination for Jaipur traditional clothing. Not just because of the quality but also the experience. Every visit has been pleasant and the product has always delivered. Highly reliable.

Bought a set of readymade ladies cotton kurtis as a joint birthday gift for twin friends. Both loved theirs and wore them to the birthday dinner. Nice to find a store that makes gifting easy with quality products.

The print quality on the block print kurtis here is noticeably better than average. The lines are crisp, the registration is accurate, and the colours are rich without bleeding into each other. Fine craft work.

I look for daily wear ethnic kurtis that are easy to maintain. Cotton from here fits that requirement perfectly — machine washable, minimal ironing needed, and holds its shape well. Practical everyday clothing.

The store had exactly what I was looking for: a kurta set for an office function that was ethnic but professional. Settled on a straight-cut cotton kurti with subtle embroidery that looked polished and appropriate.

Jaipur wholesale kurtis from here offer good margins for boutique retailers. The quality is consistent across batches and the design range means I can cater to different customer preferences. Reliable wholesale partner.

The festive wear collection here goes beyond just adding more embellishments to regular kurtis. The silhouettes, fabrics, and design motifs are all appropriate for celebration dressing. Well-curated festive range.

Wanted to buy authentic Jaipur kurtis during my trip here and a local shopkeeper pointed me to Sangrang. The authenticity is evident in every piece — genuine block prints, quality cotton, and traditional motifs. Exactly right.

The palazzo sets look beautiful on and are genuinely comfortable. Wide-leg silhouette, elastic waist, and flowing fabric make them ideal for long days. The matching kurtis complete the look without any effort.

My first purchase from here was a cotton tunic top and I've gone back many times since. The quality is consistent and the range keeps expanding. A store you can count on for reliable ethnic wear purchases.

The premium rayon pieces here feel luxurious for their price point. Good sheen, beautiful drape, and the colours are well-chosen — deep, rich tones that photograph beautifully. Bought two rayon sets and love both.

Great variety for all occasions. Whether you need something for a busy workday or a family function, the range covers both ends well. And the pricing is consistent across categories — no inflated festive pricing.

Visited on a busy Saturday and despite the footfall, the staff was attentive and the billing was quick. Good at managing a crowd without making customers feel rushed. The experience remained positive throughout.
The kurtis here have been consistently well-received when I gift them. People always comment on the quality and ask where I bought them. That's the best endorsement a product can get.

Found lovely comfortable summer cotton suit sets here — three pieces all in light, breathable cotton with matching prints. Exactly what you need in Jaipur from April through June. Cool, comfortable, and beautiful.

Bought the Jaipur cotton kurti with palazzo for my colleague's farewell gift. She loved it immediately. The print was vibrant, the fabric quality was evident, and it fit her well. Easy decision in the end.

The store displays new arrivals prominently which makes it easy to spot what's fresh. I've noticed the collection updates seasonally which keeps it interesting. Always something new to find on each visit.

Looking for stylish ready-to-wear kurtis without spending boutique prices. Found them here. The styling is thoughtful, the quality is genuine, and the prices are honest. Exactly the value proposition I was looking for.

The best brand for ladies cotton suits recommendation from a friend turned out to be very accurate. The suits here are well-made, properly finished, and have that traditional quality that's getting harder to find.

Bought a 3 piece kurti pant dupatta set for a family function and everyone loved the outfit. The coordinated look worked beautifully and the fabric was comfortable throughout the event. Good festive purchase.

The ethnic gowns here have a lovely traditional feel without being old-fashioned. Somebody clearly understands how to balance traditional craft with contemporary relevance. The result is clothing that feels both authentic and modern.

I've bought from several ethnic wear stores in Jaipur and this one consistently delivers the best combination of quality and price. Not cheap but genuinely good value. The extra investment is always justified.

The store's collection of trendy women's ethnic wear is well-curated. Nothing feels randomly thrown in — each piece has its place in the range. You can tell there's design thinking behind the collection.

Bought a beautiful Jaipuri block print gown here that's become one of my most worn pieces. Genuine hand printing, quality cotton fabric, and a silhouette that flatters many body types. Excellent purchase.

For daily use kurtis that look good without requiring effort, this is the right place. Cotton, comfortable, well-printed, and easy care. The kind of everyday garment that simplifies getting dressed in the morning.

Staff here are clearly trained well. They know the products, understand the fabrics, and give helpful advice without being condescending. That's the kind of retail experience that keeps customers coming back.

The readymade ethnic wear range covers a genuinely wide span of occasions. Daily casual, office appropriate, semi-formal, and festive — they do all four well. One stop shopping for all your ethnic wardrobe needs.

Found the exact kind of designer cotton straight kurtis I was looking for here. Minimal but interesting — just enough design detail to make them special, but clean enough for everyday wear. Exactly right.

The affordable designer kurtis here punch well above their price point. The design sensibility, fabric quality, and finishing are all better than what you'd typically expect at these prices. Great value discovery.

I travel to Jaipur often for work and always make time for a shopping stop here. The collection refreshes regularly so there's always something new. My ethnic wardrobe has benefited enormously from these visits.

The Sangrang Jaipur kurtis range is comprehensive. From the simplest daily wear cotton to elaborate festive gowns, the brand covers the full ethnic wardrobe. That breadth combined with consistent quality is impressive.

My first time buying ready made ethnic wear for women here and I was pleasantly surprised by the fit. The sizing is generous enough for comfort but tailored enough to look neat. Very happy with the purchase.

The traditional suit sets here are the kind of thing you wear repeatedly without getting tired of them. Classic patterns, quality fabric, and timeless silhouettes. Not trend-dependent clothing — genuinely enduring style.

Came for a single kurta and ended up spending time going through the whole range. The quality variation between price points is proportional and everything feels worth its price. No regrets about the extra purchases.

The ethnic wear options here for festive occasions are genuinely beautiful. Rich colours, detailed work, and fabrics that photograph well. Picked up two pieces for the upcoming wedding season and couldn't be happier.

Pure cotton kurti sets here are a genuine find. The cotton is clearly good quality — breathable, soft, and durable. After several washes the fabric still feels good and the print is still clear. Proper quality purchase.

This is genuinely one of the better stores for Jaipur ethnic wear. The range is wide, the quality is consistent, and the prices are fair. I've recommended it to several friends visiting Jaipur and everyone has been happy.

The rayon gowns here move beautifully when you walk. The fabric has a lovely fluid quality and the length is right for different occasions. Wore mine to a sangeet ceremony and felt perfectly dressed all evening.

Bought a cotton kurti set here for my trip to a hill station. Lightweight, packable, and comfortable for long days of walking and sightseeing. The print was cheerful and I wore it multiple times. Good travel clothing.

The ladies cotton tunic tops here are versatile and well-made. Long enough to wear as a dress with leggings or as a top with trousers. The fabric is soft and the fit is flattering. Several repeat purchases from me.

Seasonal collections here always reflect Jaipur's textile heritage. The festive range especially is thoughtfully designed with occasion-appropriate fabrics and embellishments. Well worth visiting before major celebrations.

First purchase was a casual cotton kurti and I was so happy with it that I went back the same week for more. The quality is genuinely impressive for the price and the range ensures there's always something suitable.

I look for comfortable kurtis that I can wear to work and then straight to evening plans. The fabrics here work for that — polished enough for meetings, comfortable enough for after-work outings. Practical wardrobe choices.

The block print gowns here use authentic motifs — peacocks, florals, geometric patterns — all executed in the traditional Jaipur way. These aren't mass-market reproductions. The craft quality is visible and genuine.

Bought stylish ready-to-wear kurtis here for an impromptu family event. No time to get anything stitched and the readymade options here were so good that I didn't need to. Quality and fit both worked out perfectly.

The store has a calm, organized shopping environment which I appreciate. No chaos, no pressure, and the staff is helpful when needed. The combination of good ambiance and great product makes it a reliable shopping destination.

The casual cotton kurtis here have become my daily uniform. Light, comfortable, and presentable enough for most situations. Easy to maintain and the quality holds across many washes. Excellent everyday purchase.

Sangrang MI Road is now my first recommendation whenever someone asks where to shop for ethnic wear in Jaipur. Consistent quality, genuine designs, and a pleasant shopping experience. Fully deserved recommendation.

Visited to find something for a formal office event and found a beautiful straight-cut cotton kurti with subtle embroidery. Professional, traditional, and comfortable. Exactly right for the occasion. Very pleased.

Bought a premium rayon kurti set for a corporate event. The fabric looked polished and professional, the colour was subdued and appropriate, and the fit was right off the rack. Impressed with the overall quality.

Found beautiful affordable designer kurtis for my trip wardrobe. The designs are sophisticated without being overdone and the prices were genuinely reasonable. Came home with five pieces and zero regret.
